seoTitle: Rocky Point vs Rose Park — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Rocky Point and Rose Park. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Rocky Point vs Rose Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Rocky Point and Rose Park.
heading: Rocky Point vs Rose Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Rocky Point, South Australia and Rose Park, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Rose Park has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Rocky Point (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Rocky Point or Rose Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Rocky Point has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Rose Park scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Rocky Point scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. Rose Park scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Rocky Point or Rose Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Rose Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
